rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE JACQUELINE WARD 2007 REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #7, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/28/25. (No earlier available date.) 1. Proof of payment of the assessment fee in the related case must be filed before an accounting can be approved. 2. All petitions involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries. [CRC 7.902.] 3. Notice was mailed to the beneficiary in San Francisco, but she is reportedly now living with her daughter in Berkeley. (The CIU report indicates that the daughter lives in Richmond?) 4. Reimbursements to a fiduciary must show who the original payee was. 5. The statements required by Probate Code Sect. 1064(a) are not fully forthcoming, as there appear to have been at least two family members paid for caregiving services. 6. Why are the Comcast bills so high for internet and TV? The beneficiary is reportedly blind. (Maurice Ward was appointed as successor trustee on 6/22/11, not 7/9/11. It would have been helpful had the petitioner disclosed that Maurice Ward's resignation was accepted by the Court on 4/19/23, especially in light of the confusing final sentence in paragraph 1, when Maurice Ward did not also sign the petition. Just because the trustee is not seeking compensation is not a reason for him to not retain receipts for purchases he made ostensibly for the benefit of the beneficiary.) Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 5/14/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TAVITA E AVEGALIO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/15/25. Examiner's Notes emailed on 3/10/25 with the following issues: 1) The interests proposed to be distributed to the issue of Seuea Avegalio, (Kenneth Kennedy, Asoula Alejo, and Donald Banford, Jr.) are still stated to be 1/8 to each beneficiary (1/24 interest in the real property). Please provide a memo of points and authorities. Please also note that Petitioner is currently proposing to distribute 10 shares of 1/8 interest in the estate. 2) A revised proposed order must be filed once all issues above have been addressed. Also, the interests of Joseph Avegalio, Tammie Arnold, and Matthew Arnold should include Christopher Avegalio's interest, rather than listing them as separate interests in the order (i.e. their total share should be 1/24 interest in the estate and 1/72 interest in the real property, each).

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF JANET DONALD PARTRIDGE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/20/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice of the amendment was mailed to the conservatee at her former residence, not the one shown in the GC-080 filed on 3/6/25 to be her residence since 12/27/24. (2) Notice was mailed to Jennifer P. Hall at an address that differs from the one that is shown in the petition and the amendment to the petition. (3) No statements appear to have been filed yet for the accounts with Computershare and AST. (4) There were payments to both Relief Home Care and Safe Eldercare Solutions for caregiver services June-August 2023, December 2023, and January 2024. Please explain why both organizations were necessary. (There were also significant payments to Relief Nursing Services between 4/26/23 - 6/3/23.) (5) For whom were flowers purchased on 12/15/22, 2/23/23, 7/10/23, and 11/30/23? (6) The attorneys received $20,786.31 statutory fees in the conservatee's husband's estate for the period of that estate's administration (4/10/21 - 12/31/23). Please explain how time billed in this case, in particular, those billed by three different billing parties on 7/12/23, was distinct from those estate administration services. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 5/6/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The procedural issues have not been cleared: (1) Petition must be verified by the fiduciary. Prob. Code sec. 1021. (2) Caption (Petition) to set forth all relief. CRC 7.102. (3) Notice (Page 1) failed to affirm this report is made under sec. 12200. Re-notice required with the correct caption/title of the petition. (4) Notice of Directions for Remote appearance and Access must be given. LRSF 14.1.C. (5) Proposed order must be submitted. LRSF 14.7.A. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHEE WANG CHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/28/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The administrator paid A People's Choice $3,975 for "probate services." There is no authority for a personal representative to pay any fees to anyone without a Court order. [CRC 7.700.] (2) Additionally, statutory fees, if payable to an attorney or the personal representative, would be a maximum of $2,577.63, based on the I&A. (3) The proposed distribution amounts appear to be slightly incorrect if cash on hand is $64,443.27. Although A People's Choice is essentially committing UPL, the administrator could amend her petition to seek statutory compensation for herself, since she is SELF-REPRESENTED. What she does with any statutory compensation awarded to her is her business. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 5/14/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARIE CHRISTINE GONELLA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/14/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Item 3e(2) of the petition does not appear to be true, as the decedent was survived by two minor children. (3) What are the ages of the minors? (4) The will is not self-proving. Proof of Subscribing Witness must be filed. (5) Not all persons mentioned in the will were listed in item 8 of the petition. (6) Alternative executors must receive notice. [Probate Code Section 8110.] Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 5/1/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
